title: rubus arcticus subsp. acaulis
intro: Rubus arcticus subsp. acaulis, also known as the stemless Arctic bramble or stemless wild rose, is a subspecies of the Arctic bramble plant, a type of rose that thrives in the Arctic and subarctic regions. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rubus arcticus subsp. acaulis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including sore throats and skin irritations.
